sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: |‘Magnificent Seven’ stocks mostly climb, with big gains for Amazon and Tesla
feed.summary: |The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks mostly saw gains on Wednesday, with Amazon and Tesla leading the way with significant climbs. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF, which holds these stocks, was up 1.7%. This performance contributed to a broader market recovery with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all bouncing back.

feed.title: AT&T Shares Rise 9.5% in a Year: Should You Invest Now?
feed.summary: AT&T (T) has seen a 9.5% rise in its shares over the past year, outperforming the Wireless National industry but lagging behind the broader tech sector and S&P 500. The company shows strong momentum in its Communications segment, driven by customer additions, a successful convergence strategy, and technological advancements like AI-native network architecture. However, AT&T faces challenges including spectrum crunch in a saturated market, high long-term debt, and stiff competition, leading Zacks to rate it as a #3 (Hold) stock.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) VP Sells 4,054 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. (NYSE:CMI) VP Amy Rochelle Davis sold 4,054 shares of the company's stock for over $2.27 million on March 3rd, reducing her position by 20.41%. This follows a previous sale of 1,864 shares in late February. The article also details Cummins' recent stock performance, financial ratios, dividend announcement, and analyst ratings, alongside institutional ownership information.

feed.title: Michele Lau Sells 2,725 Shares of McKesson (NYSE:MCK) Stock
feed.summary: Michele Lau, EVP of McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK), sold 2,725 shares of the company's stock on March 2nd for a total of $2,697,750, reducing her direct ownership by 45.63%. The company's stock traded down during midday trading, with a market cap of $121.04 billion. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" rating for McKesson with a consensus price target of $943.50.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL) Director Daniel Pope Sells 500 Shares
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL) Director Daniel Pope sold 500 shares of the company's stock on March 2nd, at an average price of $355.40, totaling $177,700.00. This transaction decreased his direct ownership by 14.83% to 2,871 shares. The company recently reported earnings per share of $2.64, missing analyst estimates, and its revenue for the quarter was $575.18 million.

feed.title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Divya Nettimi, Ray Dalio, Warren Buffett, Leopold Aschenbrenner, Squarepoint Capital, Brevan Howard, Millennium Management, SoFi Technologies Inc (SOFI), CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF), and More

It also highlights insider buying and selling activities in companies like SoFi Technologies Inc (SOFI) and CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF), and discusses investment strategies related to AI stocks and traditional assets like gold.

The short-interest ratio is 1.4 days, with approximately 1.1% of the company's shares sold short. The article also details recent insider selling, institutional investor activity, analyst ratings, and dividend increases for AMETEK.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:ZBH) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:ZBH) saw a 14.1% decrease in short interest in February, with 8.41 million shares shorted as of February 13th. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.24. Analyst ratings for ZBH are mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $106.20.

feed.title: AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T) Short Interest Down 14.2% in February
feed.summary: AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T) experienced a 14.2% drop in short interest during February, with total short interest falling to 111.65 million shares. This represents 1.6% of the company's shares being short sold, and its short-interest ratio is 2.2 days. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend, indicating positive sentiment despite some mixed analyst signals and past legacy issues.

feed.title: A Look At EPR Properties (EPR) Valuation After Strong Q4 2025 Results And Dividend Increase
feed.summary: EPR Properties (EPR) recently reported strong Q4 2025 results, increased its dividend, and updated its 2026 earnings guidance, leading to positive share price momentum. While a narrative model suggests the stock might be 2.6% overvalued at $59.86 compared to its fair value of $58.35, its P/E ratio of 18.3x is significantly lower than the industry average, indicating potential upside if market sentiment shifts. The company's strong balance sheet positions it well for future growth through accretive deals, but risks like shifting entertainment preferences remain.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Diamondback Energy (FANG) After Its Strong Recent Share Price Run
feed.summary: Diamondback Energy (FANG) has seen significant share price appreciation, raising questions about its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 64.2%, estimating an intrinsic value of $495.95 compared to the current price of $177.53. However, its P/E ratio of 30.2x is above the Oil and Gas industry average and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio of 25.1x, indicating it might be overvalued by this metric.

feed.title: Scotiabank Raises Price Target for UDR to $39.00 | UDR Stock New
feed.summary: Scotiabank has raised its price target for UDR to $39.00 from $37.00, while maintaining a "Sector Perform" rating. This 5.41% increase follows a trend of other analysts also adjusting price targets for UDR, a real estate investment trust focused on multifamily apartment communities. Wall Street analysts currently forecast an average target price of $41.07 for UDR, suggesting an upside from its current price.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Darden Restaurants (DRI) After Its Recent Share Price Strength
feed.summary: Simply Wall St's analysis suggests that Darden Restaurants (DRI) currently trades below its intrinsic value, with its shares deemed 11.3% undervalued based on a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and also undervalued when comparing its P/E ratio to fair ratio estimates. The article explores both bullish and bearish narratives for the company, projecting fair values ranging from US$169.22 to US$263.49 per share, acknowledging different investor perspectives.

feed.title: A Look At CoStar Group (CSGP) Valuation After CEO’s Recent Insider Share Purchase
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) is under review following CEO Andrew Florance's recent purchase of 55,720 shares, marking a rare insider buy after a period of sales. Despite a significant one-year share price decline and ongoing regulatory and earnings concerns, the stock is considered by some narratives to be 40.1% undervalued with a fair value of $78.05. However, its current P/S ratio of 6x is well above its real estate peer average, raising questions about valuation risk amidst optimism for future growth and potential activist pressure.

feed.title: Sempra Cancels Vista Pacífico LNG Project in the Gulf of California
feed.summary: Sempra has announced the cancellation of its proposed Vista Pacífico LNG export terminal in Topolobampo, Sinaloa, Mexico, a project that had been planned for the Gulf of California, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Natural Resources Defense Council (NRDC) commended Sempra for this decision, highlighting the project's threats to marine ecosystems, local communities, and fisheries. While this project is halted, the NRDC notes that two other LNG export terminals are still proposed for the Gulf, indicating that the fight to protect the marine sanctuary continues.

feed.title: SENEA vs. CAG: Which Packaged Foods Stock Is the Smarter Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article compares Seneca Foods Corporation (SENEA) and Conagra Brands, Inc. (CAG), highlighting their differing business models and financial performance. It concludes that Seneca Foods, with its strong momentum, improving fundamentals, and modest valuation, presents a more compelling near-term investment case compared to Conagra Brands, which faces near-term challenges impacting investor sentiment.

feed.title: Kenvue (KVUE) Valuation Revisited After Schafer Cullen Exit And Ongoing Consumer Headwinds
feed.summary: Kenvue's valuation is being re-examined after Schafer Cullen Capital Management exited its position, leading to institutional selling amidst operational headwinds. While some analysts see potential upside and a narrative suggests Kenvue is 27.7% undervalued with a fair value of $25.09, concerns remain regarding input costs, talc-related issues, and a P/E ratio that is higher than its industry and peers. Investors are left to weigh growth hopes against valuation risks to determine if the current weakness of the stock presents an entry point.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) Short Interest Down 14.3% in February
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) saw a 14.3% decrease in short interest in February, with 3.1% of shares currently short sold. The company's stock traded down $4.26 recently, with a market cap of $38.62 billion and a P/E ratio of 36.42. Vulcan Materials also recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.52 per share, and analysts have issued a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $326.14.

feed.title: 3 biggest fast food giants to close 908 locations – and the first wave is already underway
feed.summary: Three major fast food chains, Pizza Hut, Papa Johns, and Wendy's, are set to close a combined total of 908 locations due to industry pressures such as rising costs and changing consumer preferences. Pizza Hut plans to close 250 restaurants, Papa Johns anticipates shuttering 300, and Wendy's expects to close up to 358 in the first half of 2026, with some closures already underway. Other chains like Denny's, Red Lobster, TGI Friday's, Applebee's, and Noodles & Company are also planning significant closures.

feed.title: Here's Why You Should Retain Clover Stock in Your Portfolio for Now
feed.summary: Clover Health Investments (CLOV) is positioned for growth due to its technology-first care model, with strong membership growth, rising revenues, and sustained adjusted EBITDA profitability. While the company faces challenges such as elevated medical costs and execution risks, it anticipates achieving GAAP net income profitability in 2026, benefiting from a 4-Star CMS rating and strategic cost management. Despite regulatory pressures and the need to scale its tech platforms, Zacks Equity Research recommends retaining CLOV stock given its long-term potential.

feed.title: Mass. biotech giants added workers in 2025 despite industry-wide layoffs
feed.summary: Despite industry-wide layoffs dominating headlines, several major Massachusetts life sciences companies actually increased their headcount in 2025. This article highlights specific companies that hired and those that reduced their workforce within the state's robust biotech sector. It indicates that some firms, like Acton-based Insulet and Vertex, experienced growth while others, such as Moderna and Sarepta, saw job cuts.

feed.title: OPXS Skyrockets 140% in a Year: Should You Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Optex Systems Holdings, Inc. (OPXS) shares have surged 139.5% over the past year, outperforming competitors due to strong defense demand, a growing order backlog, and specialized expertise in military optical systems. Despite facing challenges like rising raw material costs and supply chain disruptions, the company is considered undervalued compared to industry averages and its peers. This combination of strong fundamentals and low valuation presents a potentially lucrative opportunity for investors.

feed.title: Investors Are Bullish on Alphabet Stock - Piling Into GOOGL Call Options With Huge Unusual Options Volume
feed.summary: Investors are showing a strong bullish sentiment towards Alphabet (GOOGL) stock, evident by a significant surge in call option purchases. Despite an 11.5% drop from its pre-earnings peak, analysts and free cash flow projections suggest the stock is undervalued, with potential price targets significantly higher than its current trading price. The article highlights unusual options activity, specifically a high volume of in-the-money call options expiring soon, indicating traders anticipate GOOGL will remain above the strike price.

feed.title: Estate lawyer joins Eastern Bank to guide special needs families
feed.summary: Eastern Bank has appointed Judith L. Bomster, J.D., as a Senior Vice President, Senior Trust Officer with Cambridge Trust Wealth Management. Bomster brings over 25 years of legal experience, specializing in trust and estate administration and special needs planning. Her expertise will enhance the services provided to families needing complex estate and generational planning, especially those with vulnerable beneficiaries.

feed.title: Boeing CFO Jay Malave to Speak at Bank of America Global Industrials Conference March 17
feed.summary: Boeing Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Jay Malave is scheduled to speak at the Bank of America Global Industrials Conference on March 17 at 9:50 a.m. ET. A webcast and transcript of the event will be available on the Investors section of Boeing's website. Participants are advised to verify webcast access prior to the event.

feed.title: Scotiabank Lowers Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) Price Target to $140.00
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price target for Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) to $140.00 from $142.00, maintaining a "sector perform" rating. This adjustment comes after MAA reported a significant quarterly EPS miss, though revenue was in line with expectations. Analyst sentiment remains mixed, with a consensus price target of $152.95 despite recent target cuts from several firms.

feed.title: Invitation Home (NYSE:INVH) Price Target Cut to $27.00 by Analysts at Scotiabank
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price target for Invitation Home (NYSE:INVH) from $28.00 to $27.00, maintaining a "sector perform" rating, which implies a limited upside of 1.73%. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst ratings, with an overall consensus target of $33.29, despite the company missing quarterly EPS expectations but largely meeting revenue forecasts. Institutional interest in INVH remains strong, with significant buying activity reported from major financial institutions, leading to nearly 97% institutional ownership.

feed.title: Apollo’s Zito Sees Private Credit Pain Lasting Up to 18 Months
feed.summary: John Zito of Apollo Global Management Inc. anticipates that the current difficulties in private credit markets could persist for another 12 to 18 months. Despite this, Zito, co-president of Apollo's asset management arm, stated that he does not view the recent withdrawals from some private credit funds as a significant issue. He shared these insights during the Bloomberg Invest conference in New York.

feed.title: Can Take-Two Interactive Stock Beat the Market?
feed.summary: Take-Two Interactive's stock has faced pressure due to valuation pullbacks and "Grand Theft Auto VI" delays, coupled with AI fears. The upcoming release of "Grand Theft Auto VI" in November 2026 is anticipated to be a major catalyst, potentially driving significant growth and allowing the company to outperform the market in the next five years, despite recent underperformance compared to major indices. The article suggests the game's long product life cycle and a softening competitive landscape could contribute to its success.

feed.title: Sharp new SVP of L3Harris Technologies
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies has appointed Kenneth “Ken” Sharp as its new Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective March 16, 2026. Sharp brings over 30 years of financial leadership experience in defense and technology. Concurrently, Kenneth “Ken” Bedingfield will now exclusively lead the Missile Solutions segment, focusing on scaling solid rocket motor manufacturing and preparing for the segment's eventual initial public offering.

feed.title: Inside PPG, now a four-time winner of Gallup's top workplace honor
feed.summary: PPG (NYSE: PPG) has received its fourth consecutive Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award, recognizing its highly engaged workplace culture. The company achieved record-setting employee engagement in 2025, driven by initiatives like employee workshops, community projects, leadership development, and expanded onboarding. This award highlights PPG's commitment to fostering a thriving and high-performing global organization.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma names chief marketing officer
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma has promoted Abby Teisch to Chief Marketing Officer. Teisch, a 20-year veteran of the company, will now oversee performance marketing, customer engagement, and data-driven growth initiatives across all Williams-Sonoma brands. Her appointment is expected to accelerate the company's long-term growth strategies and leverage her expertise in digital marketing and customer loyalty.

feed.title: Lawmakers Question Intel’s Use of Tools From Blacklisted Chinese Firm
feed.summary: A bipartisan group of senators has raised national security concerns regarding Intel's potential use of tools from ACM Research, a blacklisted Chinese semiconductor company. This scrutiny is amplified by the fact that U.S. taxpayers now partially own Intel, following a significant government investment. The lawmakers are demanding answers from Intel's CEO about safeguards against national security risks and the flow of taxpayer money to China.

feed.title: Old Dominion Unveils Weak LTL Unit Performance for February
feed.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) reported a 3.3% year-over-year decline in daily revenues for its less-than-truckload (LTL) segment in February 2026, primarily due to a 6.8% decrease in LTL tons per day. Despite the weakness, the company's president expressed cautious optimism about the domestic economy and the company's ability to manage future volume increases due to its "best-in-class service" and disciplined approach to yield management. ODFL holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and has outperformed the transportation-truck industry over the past three months.

feed.title: Cummins (NYSE: CMI) VP Amy Davis sells 4,054 shares in open market
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. VP Amy Rochelle Davis sold 4,054 shares of company stock in six separate open-market transactions on March 3, 2026. The shares were sold at weighted average prices in the mid-$560s, ranging from $559.61 to $566.14 per share. Following these sales, she directly holds 15,807.013 Cummins common shares and maintains direct holdings of multiple stock option awards.

feed.title: Lau Michele, McKesson Corp EVP, sells $2.69m in stock
feed.summary: Michele Lau, Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer at McKesson Corp (NYSE:MCK), sold 2,725 shares of common stock for $2,697,750 on March 2, 2026. This sale, executed under a Rule 10b5-1(c) plan, occurred while the stock was trading near its 52-week high, reflecting a strong performance. The company recently reported better-than-expected Q3 fiscal 2026 financial results, with adjusted EPS and revenue exceeding analyst forecasts.

feed.title: [Form 4] CHURCH & DWIGHT CO INC /DE/ Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Carlos Ruiz Rabago, EVP and Chief Supply Chain Officer of Church & Dwight Co Inc /DE/, reported a Form 4 transaction for 62 shares of common stock withheld at $103.02 per share to cover tax obligations from vested restricted stock units. His updated holdings now include previously granted RSUs, vested shares, direct holdings, and a small indirect position via a profit-sharing plan. This transaction reflects a tax-related withholding of shares rather than a discretionary sale or purchase.

feed.title: [144] MOODYS CORP /DE/ SEC Filing
feed.summary: A Form 144 SEC filing for Moody's Corp (MCO) reports an insider sale by Michael L. West of 3,327 common shares on February 26, 2026, totaling $1,577,340.34 over the past three months. The filing also details multiple restricted stock vesting events between 2016 and 2025. These are routine disclosures providing transparent information regarding affiliate transactions and share vesting.

feed.title: Looking Into Eversource Energy's Recent Short Interest
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) has seen a 33.14% increase in its short interest as a percent of float since its last report, with 6.60 million shares now sold short, representing 2.25% of its tradable shares. This trend suggests increased bearish sentiment among investors, though it doesn't guarantee a near-term stock decline. Compared to its peers, Eversource Energy has less short interest on average, with the peer group average at 4.36%.

feed.title: Exclusive look at what it takes to become the next big country star
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Co. has announced Gloria Anderson as the winner of its 2026 Life Out Here Emerging Artist Program. Anderson will open for Cody Johnson and record a song at Blackbird Studio, receiving mentorship and industry insights. Experts advised aspiring artists to build community, act like a CEO, and prioritize ticket sales and live performances over social media metrics.

feed.title: Humana partners with Disabled American Veterans for the sixth year
feed.summary: Humana has renewed its partnership with Disabled American Veterans (DAV) for the sixth consecutive year, demonstrating its continued commitment to supporting veterans and their families. This collaboration aims to expand resources, access, and care for the veteran community, aligning with Humana's mission to address health-related challenges like hunger, homelessness, and financial strain. DAV, a nonprofit organization with nearly 1 million members, assists over 1 million veterans annually with benefits, employment, and support services.

feed.title: Molina Healthcare (MOH): Morgan Stanley Lowers Price Target | MO
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Molina Healthcare (MOH) from $158 to $128, while maintaining an Equal-Weight rating. This adjustment follows similar reductions from several other major analysts, including Wells Fargo, Truist Securities, Goldman Sachs, Barclays, and Cantor Fitzgerald. Despite these revisions, the average one-year price target from 16 analysts is $153.59, suggesting a slight upside from the current price, with a consensus "Hold" recommendation from 20 brokerage firms.

feed.title: Tax withholding trims Church & Dwight (NYSE: CHD) EVP stock stake
feed.summary: Church & Dwight's EVP and Chief Digital Growth Officer, Surabhi Pokhriyal, reported a tax-withholding disposition of 39 shares of common stock at $103.02 per share. This action was taken to cover tax obligations incurred due to the vesting of restricted stock units. Following this transaction, Pokhriyal directly holds 381 shares, with additional indirect holdings through a savings and profit-sharing plan.

feed.title: Iron Mountain: ALM, data centers, and digital solutions drive robust growth, with ALM set to lead revenue
feed.summary: Iron Mountain is evolving into a tech-enabled infrastructure provider, experiencing double-digit growth across its ALM, data centers, and digital solutions segments. ALM is rapidly scaling and is projected to become the largest revenue segment. The company's data centers are benefiting from strong demand, while its digital and records businesses provide stable recurring revenue, supporting capital allocation and dividend growth.

feed.title: DIPS Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:DIPS
feed.summary: The article provides a detailed overview of the YieldMax Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (DIPS), including its current price, performance metrics, and key statistics like AUM and dividend yield. It explains DIPS's strategy of pursuing monthly income and inverse exposure to Nvidia stock through a synthetic covered put strategy using options. The fund does not directly invest in NVDA stocks but uses call and put options and short-term US Treasury securities.

feed.title: Rhinebeck Bancorp, Inc. Hits New 52-Week High at $16.44
feed.summary: Rhinebeck Bancorp, Inc. has reached a new 52-week high of $16.44, representing a 110.25% increase over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. The regional bank has a market capitalization of $169 million, a return on equity of 7.34%, and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.00, demonstrating strong performance and a conservative financial approach. This milestone highlights the company's notable growth and resilience within the regional banking sector.

feed.title: Is New Oriental Education & Technology Group (EDU) Attractively Priced After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: New Oriental Education & Technology Group (EDU) is currently trading at US$52.55, and analysis suggests potential undervaluation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicates a 22.3% discount, pointing to an intrinsic value of about $67.61 per share. Although its P/E ratio of 21.98x is higher than the industry average, Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio of 27.39x suggests the stock might be undervalued when considering its specific growth and risk profile.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser stock: why timber REITs are back on watchlists for 2026
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE: WY), a timber-focused REIT, is gaining attention from investors due to its unique position at the intersection of housing, interest rates, and inflation. The company provides a macro bet on U.S. homebuilding and long-term lumber demand, acting as a hybrid between a cyclical industrial stock and an inflation-sensitive real asset. Recent price action and analyst moves suggest a shifting risk-reward profile for 2026 as investors consider WY's exposure to timberlands, real estate, and wood products, alongside its dividend profile and potential in emerging carbon markets.

feed.title: Waste Management (NYSE: WM) CIO sells 617 shares in open-market trade
feed.summary: Waste Management Inc.'s SVP and CIO, Johnson Varkey, sold 617 shares of common stock at an average price of $244.13 per share on March 3, 2026. Following this transaction, Varkey directly owns 9,606.7856 shares of the company. The sale was reported in a Form 4 SEC filing, indicating a moderate filing impact and negative sentiment.

feed.title: Ventas at Raymond James Conference: Strategic Growth in Senior Housing
feed.summary: Ventas Inc. presented at the 47th Annual Raymond James Institutional Investor Conference, detailing its strategic focus on senior housing. The company is leveraging demographic trends, aiming for double-digit NOI growth, and has invested over $5 billion in the sector. Ventas highlighted its Operational Insights platform and aggressive acquisition strategy to drive future growth and shareholder value.
